title: Section 88
content: For the purpose of sections 86 and 87, a "superior military authority " means-- (a) in the case of punishments awarded by a commanding officer, any officer superior in command to such commanding officer; (b) in the case of punishments awarded by any other authority, the Central Government, 1[the Chief of the Army Staff] or other officer specified by 1[the Chief of the Army Staff].
footnote: 1. Subs. by Act 19 of 1955, s. 2 and the Schedule, for the "Commander-in-Chief".
